Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. 15 Years of Forking 160 upvotes, 22 comments
  2. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 299 upvotes, 52 comments
  3. Interview: Nobonoko, Master of the Minimal Sequencer 29 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 134 upvotes, 26 comments
  5. HD Audio Driver for Windows 98SE / Me 41 upvotes, 5 comments
  6. About the Atmosphere 56 upvotes, 9 comments
  7. 15 years, one server, 8GB RAM and 500k users – how Webminal refuses to die 81 upvotes, 18 comments
  8. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 490 upvotes, 157 comments
  9. Douglas Lenat's Automated Mathematician Source Code 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 520 upvotes, 200 comments
  11. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 227 upvotes, 80 comments
  12. Hardware Image Compression 21 upvotes, 4 comments
  13. The Cognitive Dark Forest 421 upvotes, 189 comments
  14. ninja: a small build system with a focus on speed 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. Eclipse GlassFish: This Isn't Your Father's GlassFish 17 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. The road signs that teach travellers about France 98 upvotes, 48 comments
  17. VHDL's Crown Jewel 49 upvotes, 21 comments
  18. Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman Equation: Reinforcement Learning and Diffusion Models 18 upvotes, 5 comments
  19. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 611 upvotes, 395 comments
  20. I use excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 35 upvotes, 14 comments
  21. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era 31 upvotes, 15 comments
  22. How to Survive in the Tech industry in 2026 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  23. "Roadrunner": a bipedal, wheeled robot for multi-modal locomotion [video] 28 upvotes, 14 comments
  24. When Coupled Volcanoes Talk, These Researchers Listen 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. C++26 is done: ISO C++ standards meeting Trip Report 227 upvotes, 195 comments
  26. Midnight train from GA: A view of America from the tracks as airports struggle 93 upvotes, 76 comments
  27. Gonon: Building a Clock with No Numerals 28 upvotes, 18 comments
  28. Coding Agents Could Make Free Software Matter Again 167 upvotes, 163 comments
  29. My MacBook keyboard is broken and it's insanely expensive to fix 177 upvotes, 213 comments
  30. AI and bots have officially taken over the internet 24 upvotes, 39 comments

Last generated 5:00pm, 30 March 2026. Source here.
